verses.t: Then Azariah stood and offered this prayer; in the midst of the fire he opened his mouth and said:
"Baruk art thou, 0 Yahuah, Aluahim of our fathers, and worthy of praise; and thy name is glorified for ever.
For thou art just in all that thou hast done to us, and all thy works are true and thy ways right, and all thy mishpatim are truth.

Thou hast executed true mishpatim in all that thou hast brought upon us and upon Yerushalayim, the holy city of our fathers, for in truth and justice thou hast brought all this upon us because of our sins.
For we have sinfully and lawlessly departed from thee, and have sinned in all things and have not obeyed thy command ments;
we have not observed them or done them, as thou hast commanded us that it might go well with us.
So all that thou hast brought upon us, and all that thou hast done to us, thou hast done in true mishpat.
Thou hast given us into the hands of lawless enemies, most hateful rebels, and to an unjust king, the most wicked in all the world.
And now we cannot open our mouths; shame and disgrace have befallen thy servants and worshipers.
For thy name's sake do not give us up utterly, and do not break thy berith,
and do not withdraw thy mercy from us, for the sake of Aḇraham thy beloved and for the sake of Yitsḥaq thy servant and Yasharal thy holy one,
to whom thou didst promise to make their descendants as many as the stars of shamayim and as the sand on the shore of the sea.
For we, 0 Yahuah, have become fewer than any nation, and are brought low this day in all the world because of our sins.
And at this time there is no prince, or naḇi, or leader, no burnt offering, or sacrifice, or oblation, or incense, no place to make an offering before thee or to find mercy.
Yet with a contrite heart and a humble ruach may we be accepted, as though it were with burnt offerings of rams and bulls, and with tens of thousands of fat lambs;
such may our sacrifice be in thy sight this day, and may we wholly follow thee, for there will be no shame for those who trust in thee.

And now with all our heart we follow thee, we fear thee and seek thy face. [19J Do not put us to shame, but deal with us in thy forbearance and in thy abundant mercy. [20J Deliver us in accordance with thy marvelous works, and give glory to thy name, 0 Yahuah! Let all who do harm to thy servants be put to shame; [21 J let them be disgraced and deprived of all power and dominion, and let their strength be broken.

Let them know that thou art Yahuah, the only Aluahim, glorious over the whole world." [23J Now the king's servants who threw them in did not cease feeding the furnace fires with naphtha, pitch, tow, and brush. [24J And the flame streamed out above the furnace forty-nine cubits, [25J and it broke through and burned those of the Chaldeans whom it caught about the furnace. [26J But the mal'ak of Yahuah came down into the furnace to be with Azariah and his companions, and drove the fiery flame out of the furnace, [27J and made the midst of the furnace like a moist whistling wind, so that the fire did not touch them at all or hurt or trouble them. [28J Then the three, as with one mouth, praised and glorified and baruk Aluahim in the furnace, saying:
